description: Generic show / hide primitive. CollapsibleTrigger is a real <button> with aria-expanded and aria-controls pointing at the CollapsibleContent region; Radix keeps them in sync. Unlike Accordion, there is no roving tabindex, arrow-key navigation, or group context.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the trigger and then into the revealed content when expanded.

focus.notes: The trigger uses whatever focus ring the consumer supplies (it's an unstyled <button> until styled). Focus is never trapped inside the content.

announcements: The trigger announces its current expanded / collapsed state.
Content appears / disappears in the accessibility tree as it mounts / unmounts.
screenReader.notes: Collapsible exposes no role for its content by default; consumers who need "region" semantics should add role="region" and aria-labelledby themselves.

contrast.text: Inherits from consumer-provided children; no default text tokens
contrast.ui: No chrome; consumer supplies all visuals

motion.reducedMotion: Radix exposes --radix-collapsible-content-height / -width CSS variables but does not animate by default. Any transition is authored by the consumer and should be gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.

consumerResponsibilities: Style the trigger as a real button; pair it with an icon (chevron) whose rotation mirrors the open state.
When the collapsed content is a distinct section (FAQ answer, optional panel), add role="region" and aria-labelledby so screen readers can navigate to it.
Avoid nesting interactive focusables that are visually hidden when collapsed; Radix unmounts the content, so they leave the tab order cleanly.
